div.h1: Logical Operators: Shell vs C Precedence

p.em: Technical Specification of Parser Associativity and Execution Flow

g.text: Total Structure Size: 32 Bytes (Half Cache Line)

div.p: When writing Shell scripts: NEVER mix && and || without explicit grouping.
Shell parses A || B && C as (A || B) && C.
Observation: In Shell, the && does not "pull" the false towards it. It evaluates the result of (true || false) and then evaluates &&.

ol.li: Because LHS is true, the || operator short-circuits.
The entire RHS branch (false && action()) is never visited.
